prompt:
An adjustable dual-arm helping hands tool with a weighted base stands on a soldering mat, its two metal arms ending in small alligator clips that hold a PCB and a loose wire in alignment. The base is compact and heavy-looking, with a simple, unbranded finish, and the arms are articulated with thumb screws at each joint so they can be positioned precisely. A soldering iron tip and small spool of solder are visible nearby, clearly indicating that this third-hand aid is used during soldering.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk electronics workspace with soft, even lighting, a brass tip cleaner and flux pen in the blurred background, and a small plant adding a bit of green on a shelf. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on the tool, and a composition that centers the helping hands, clipped parts, and base while the rest of the bench remains softly out of focus.
